name: 48_Stock_Trading_Simulation_PPO_HistoricalData_RL
query: ¡Hola! Estoy interesado en desarrollar un agente de trading de acciones utilizando el algoritmo Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). La idea es usar datos históricos del mercado para el entrenamiento y las pruebas. Se debe implementar un entorno de simulación de trading de acciones en `src/env.py`. El algoritmo Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) debe implementarse en `src/train.py`. Por favor, guarda el agente entrenado en `models/saved_models/`. Registra todas las decisiones de trading en `results/trade_decisions.txt` y guarda el beneficio total en `results/metrics/total_profit.txt`. Visualiza la curva de beneficios y guárdala como `results/figures/profit_curve.png`. Genera un informe que cubra la estrategia de trading, el beneficio y el análisis de riesgos, y guárdalo como `results/trading_strategy_report.md`. Implementa una herramienta interactiva usando Streamlit en `src/visualize.py` que permita a los usuarios probar diferentes parámetros y ejecutar simulaciones.

requirements.criteria: Se implementa un entorno de simulación de trading de acciones en `src/env.py`.
Se utilizan datos históricos del mercado para el entrenamiento y las pruebas.
El algoritmo "Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)" se implementa en `src/train.py`.
Las decisiones de trading se registran y guardan como `results/trade_decisions.txt`.
El beneficio total se guarda como `results/metrics/total_profit.txt`.
La curva de beneficios se visualiza y guarda como `results/figures/profit_curve.png`.
Se genera un informe que contiene la estrategia de trading, el beneficio y el análisis de riesgos, y se guarda como `results/trading_strategy_report.md`.
Se implementa una herramienta interactiva que permite a los usuarios probar diferentes parámetros y ejecutar simulaciones usando "Streamlit" en `src/visualize.py`.

preferences.criteria: La visualización de la curva de beneficios debe resaltar operaciones o eventos significativos que impactaron el rendimiento.
El informe debe incluir ideas sobre cómo el ajuste de parámetros afecta el resultado del trading.

judge_stats.criteria: Se implementa un entorno de simulación de trading de acciones en `src/env.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/env.py` file does not implement a stock trading simulation environment. Instead, it contains a placeholder/stub implementation with a `Gridworld` class (a simple grid navigation environment) and metadata strings listing requirements. There is no stock trading logic, no price data handling, no portfolio/position management, no buy/sell/hold action space, and no reward function based on trading profits. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a genuine stock trading environment.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan datos históricos del mercado para el entrenamiento y las pruebas.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/train.py` file contains only a "minimal semantic implementation" that does not use any historical market data. The `train()` function is a trivial loop (`score += step`) with no data loading, no market data processing, and no actual trading simulation. There is no reference to any dataset, CSV files, financial APIs, or historical price data anywhere in the codebase. The artifacts (e.g., `trade_decisions.txt`, `total_profit.txt`) are generated with placeholder text ("generated by minimal semantic train.py") rather than from actual training on historical data. The `env.py` file content is not shown but there is no evidence it loads real market data either.

judge_stats.criteria: El algoritmo "Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)" se implementa en `src/train.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/train.py` file does not implement the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm. Instead, it is explicitly labeled as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and contains only a trivial training loop (`score += step`) with no PPO-related components such as policy networks, value functions, clipped surrogate objectives, advantage estimation, or any reinforcement learning logic. The file merely lists PPO as a keyword in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS` variables without actually implementing it.

judge_stats.criteria: Las decisiones de trading se registran y guardan como `results/trade_decisions.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/trade_decisions.txt` exists, but its content is just a placeholder string: `('placeholder\r\n', None)`. This indicates that no actual trading decisions were recorded or saved — the file was created but not populated with real trade decision data from the PPO simulation. A properly functioning implementation should contain meaningful trading decisions (e.g., buy/sell/hold actions with timestamps, prices, or other relevant trade information).

judge_stats.criteria: El beneficio total se guarda como `results/metrics/total_profit.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/total_profit.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` — a placeholder string rather than an actual computed total profit value. This indicates the file was created but never populated with a real numerical result from the trading simulation. The criterion requires saving the actual total profit, not a placeholder.

judge_stats.criteria: La curva de beneficios se visualiza y guarda como `results/figures/profit_curve.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/profit_curve.png` exists in the project structure, as confirmed by the workspace tree which explicitly lists `results\figures\profit_curve.png`. The file was generated by `src/visualize.py` and saved at the expected path. The error shown in the file content is a reading/display error from the evaluation tool (related to image format detection), not an indication that the file is missing or corrupt — the file is present at the correct location as required.

judge_stats.criteria: Se genera un informe que contiene la estrategia de trading, el beneficio y el análisis de riesgos, y se guarda como `results/trading_strategy_report.md`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/trading_strategy_report.md` exists in the workspace, but its content is `('placeholder', None)` — a Python tuple representation string, not an actual report. It does not contain any trading strategy description, profit analysis, or risk analysis sections. The file is essentially empty/invalid as a meaningful report.

judge_stats.criteria: Se implementa una herramienta interactiva que permite a los usuarios probar diferentes parámetros y ejecutar simulaciones usando "Streamlit" en `src/visualize.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The content of `src/visualize.py` does not implement a Streamlit interactive tool. Instead, it contains only a minimal stub/placeholder that defines some metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `main()` function that simply returns a dictionary with status "ok". There is no Streamlit import, no UI components, no parameter widgets, and no simulation logic whatsoever. The file explicitly acknowledges it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a real implementation.
